steps.title: Apply the graph to the hypothesis
steps.content: The hypothesis predicts that paired leaves should tend to show opposite-side orientation. Supporting data must show that this pattern appears across the grapevine species tested, not just in one species or in a comparison unrelated to same-side orientation.

steps.title: Remove unsupported graph claims
steps.content: Choice A compares July grape and graybark grape but leaves out frost grape. Choice B emphasizes same-side orientation, which is not the predicted pattern. Choice D says all leaf pairs show opposite-side orientation, an absolute claim the graph does not support. The answer is C.
